nameMeanings.name: Gifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund Gifford of Sherifhall was born in 1335, in Scotland, his father, John Giffard, was 45 and his mother, Catherine Stafford, was 36. He had at least 3 sons with Agnes Whyston. He died in 1378, in his hometown, at the age of 43.

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund Gifford of Sherifhall(이)가 1335년 스컷랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Giffard(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Stafford(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Agnes Whyston(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1378년 그의 고향에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.
